Tractor Burden Test Simulation

Taotao Tan

12/21/2021

Here we are simulation a dataset to test Tractor burden test method

pre-defined parameters

# sample size
num_sample = 5000
num_vars = 100

# simulate global admixture proportion with a beta distribution, E(admix) = 0.8
admix = rbeta(num_sample, 8, 2)

# minor allele frequency
AFR_maf = 0.05
EUR_maf = 0.05

# how many risk alleles are responsible for the phenotype
num_risk_alleles = 3

# effect size for AFR, EUR, and global admixture 
AFR_eff_size = 0
EUR_eff_size = 2
admix_eff_size = 1.2

# error term size
error_eff_size = 2


plot(density(admix), main = "distribution of percentage of admixture")

We assume the population is two way admixed with 80% AFR and 20% EUR in average

This could be modeled with a beta distribution \(\text{Beta}(\alpha = 8, \beta = 2)\). The expected proportion of AFR compoents are 80%. (Is that a reasonable distribution to draw from?)

# generate AFR specific hap count 
AFR_hapcount = data.frame(t(sapply(admix, function(x){
  return(rbinom(num_vars, size = 2, prob = x))
})))

# generate EUR specific hap count
EUR_hapcount = 2 - AFR_hapcount

Generate dosage of risk alleles based on: hap count, ancestry-specific probability of having risk alleles

Generate_dosage = function(sample, ancestry){
  return(sapply(sample, function(x){return(rbinom(1, size = x, prob = ancestry))}))
}

# generate AFR risk allele dosage
AFR_dosage = data.frame(t(apply(AFR_hapcount,1, Generate_dosage, AFR_maf)))

# generate EUR risk allele dosage
EUR_dosage = data.frame(t(apply(EUR_hapcount,1, Generate_dosage, EUR_maf)))

# generate TOT (total) copies of risk allele dosage
TOT_dosage = AFR_dosage + EUR_dosage




hist(rowSums(AFR_dosage), main = "distribution of total AFR risk allele dosage")

hist(rowSums(EUR_dosage), main = "distribution of total EUR risk allele dosage")

hist(rowSums(TOT_dosage), main = "distribution of total risk allele dosage")

risk_alleles = sample(num_vars, size =num_risk_alleles, replace = F)
admix_linear = log(admix/(1-admix))  # is this transformation necessary?
error_term = rnorm(num_sample, sd = error_eff_size)


y = rowSums(AFR_dosage[,risk_alleles] * AFR_eff_size) + rowSums(EUR_dosage[,risk_alleles] * EUR_eff_size) + admix_linear * admix_eff_size + error_term

Modeling

# library(plotly)
df = data.frame(cbind(rowSums(AFR_dosage), rowSums(EUR_dosage), rowSums(TOT_dosage), admix, y))
colnames(df) = c("AFR", "EUR", "TOT", "admix", "pheno")
# plot_ly(df, x = ~AFR, y = ~EUR, z = ~pheno, color = ~admix, colors = c('#BF382A', '#0C4B8E'))
df$admix_linear = log(admix/(1-admix))

summary(lm(pheno ~ AFR + EUR + admix_linear, data = df))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = pheno ~ AFR + EUR + admix_linear, data = df)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -8.5072 -1.3698  0.0043  1.3939  7.4165 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.009523   0.117313   0.081   0.9353    
## AFR          0.008293   0.010334   0.803   0.4223    
## EUR          0.047478   0.019935   2.382   0.0173 *  
## admix_linear 1.152180   0.042417  27.163   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.036 on 4996 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1904, Adjusted R-squared:   0.19 
## F-statistic: 391.7 on 3 and 4996 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
summary(lm(pheno~TOT + admix_linear, data = df))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = pheno ~ TOT + admix_linear, data = df)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -8.5253 -1.3587  0.0013  1.3923  7.4484 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.087183   0.108886   0.801    0.423    
## TOT          0.016183   0.009332   1.734    0.083 .  
## admix_linear 1.103374   0.032323  34.135   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.036 on 4997 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1899, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1896 
## F-statistic: 585.8 on 2 and 4997 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
